skills.name: ai-models-backend-platform
must_include: No backend entity leaked as public platform contract.
No backend/storage/auth change without matching module-shell and config

skills.name: controller-architecture-discipline
must_include: keep them under the same LOC discipline as production files

skills.name: controller-runtime-implementation
must_include: Prefer the repository's primary implementation language for durable
If the controller package map or controller test tree changes, sync:

skills.name: dkp-module-shell
must_include: No shell/build change without matching render/verify evidence.

skills.name: k8s-api-design
must_include: No internal transport/storage/output format leaked into public `spec`.
No placeholder API knob kept “for later”.
For DKP user-facing resources, state RBAC access-level coverage

skills.name: model-catalog-api
must_include: dead public knobs with no live controller semantics

skills.name: module-config-contract
must_include: No dead public knobs without live semantics.
No fake compatibility layers that outlive the migration they were added for.

skills.name: platform-runtime-integration
must_include: No local convenience option that bypasses a DKP global mechanism
Keep DKP user-facing RBAC coverage aligned
No observability contract that exists only in logs or chat

skills.name: review-gate
must_include: check them as one instruction system
no lower-level file contradicts a higher-level rule
for any DKP user-facing API/auth/exposure/RBAC/runtime entrypoint change

skills.name: task-intake-and-slicing
must_include: make it a dedicated governance bundle
archive stale/finished or oversized active bundles

skills.name: third-party-component-integration
must_include: No patching upstream files in-place without explicit patch/rebase discipline.
No hidden source-of-truth split between vendored copy, build shell, and docs.

agents.name: api_designer
must_include: Treat dead public knobs, internal output-format leakage, and unstable conditions as real findings

agents.name: backend_integrator
must_include: Treat backend convenience leaking into public platform behavior as a real finding.

agents.name: integration_architect
must_include: always answer the byte-path, copy-count, and runtime-footprint questions explicitly.
For RBAC changes, review Deckhouse access-level wiring

agents.name: module_implementer
must_include: Do not treat repo-local workflow governance edits (
they need their own bundle and explicit consistency review.

agents.name: repo_architect
must_include: include governance-surface consistency in the review

agents.name: reviewer
must_include: review them as one instruction system and treat contradictions across layers as real findings.

agents.name: task_framer
must_include: archive stale or oversized active bundles instead of creating sibling sources of truth.
For DKP user-facing API/auth/RBAC/exposure/runtime entrypoint tasks
require consistency checks across all touched instruction layers.
Repo-local workflow rules имеют строгий precedence:
Files that must be reviewed and rewritten during porting:
что считается reusable core и что остаётся project-specific overlay;
Для DKP user-facing API/auth/RBAC/exposure/runtime entrypoint tasks дополнительно:
Для workflow/governance tasks сюда входит `make lint-codex-governance`.
это governance task, а не incidental docs cleanup;
